Abdelrahman Hassan is a Data Science Consultant with contact information including email, phone number, GitHub, LinkedIn, and personal website.

Abdelrahman Hassan received a PGDM in Business Analytics from Great Lakes Institute of Management & Illinois Institute of Technology from January 2017 to January 2018 and a Bachelor of Engineering in Electronics and Communication from New Horizon College of Engineering, Visvesvaraya Technological University in January 2013, Bengaluru, Karnataka, India.

From March 2019 to October 2019, Deloitte USI FMCG worked on Based Planning for Flowers Foods, setting up CRM and CBP modules, performing data decomposition, and establishing a foundation for future forecasts. They also developed analytical models and improved performance.

From November 2019 to January 2020, Deloitte USI FMCG performed Customer Segmentation and RFM Analysis, establishing a HANA-Python interface and using K-means clustering to segment customer data. They also developed an algorithm for automatic customer categorization.
From February 2020 to February 2020, Deloitte USI Telecom developed a decision tree model to classify invoices, reducing manual verification effort by 60%.

From October 2014 to April 2016, Accenture Solutions Pvt. Ltd. developed the SAP analytics tool (IPA) by designing database objects and data models. They also developed Finance-related KPIs and introduced SAP Predictive Analytics.

From January 2013 to September 2014, Accenture Solutions Pvt. Ltd. managed development and deployments for the ATAS tool, analyzing business requirements and designing data flow diagrams. They also created and updated database objects and performed SQL performance tuning.
The skills listed include programming languages (Python, R, C#), databases/tools (SAP HANA, MS SQL Server, Tableau, etc.), and data science techniques (Machine Learning, Deep Learning, NLP, etc.).
From February 2018 to March 2020, Abdelrahman Hassan worked as a Data Science Consultant at Deloitte USI, providing advanced analytics and leading project teams.
From October 2014 to April 2016, Abdelrahman Hassan was a Senior Developer at Accenture Solutions Pvt. Ltd., India, specializing in SAP analytics tool development.
From January 2013 to September 2014, Abdelrahman Hassan was a Database Developer at Accenture Solutions Pvt. Ltd., India, responsible for database development and maintenance.
From February 2018 to April 2018, Abdelrahman Hassan developed sentence correction functionality for a handwriting recognition system at Deloitte USI.
From May 2018 to December 2018, Abdelrahman Hassan developed an analytics infrastructure on SAP S/4 at Deloitte USI for financial reports and expense forecasting.
From January 2019 to February 2019, Abdelrahman Hassan designed and implemented an analytics infrastructure on Argus at Deloitte USI for faster querying and advanced analytics.

Abdelrahman Hassan is a driven data science consultant, readily connecting with clients and colleagues via abdelrahman.hassan@email.com, +20 101 234 5678, and profiles on GitHub, LinkedIn, and his personal website, abdelrahmanhassan.com. His career trajectory reveals a consistent upward climb, beginning with a foundation in engineering. He earned a Bachelor of Engineering in Electronics and Communication from New Horizon College of Engineering in Bengaluru, India, before refining his analytical skillset with a PGDM in Business Analytics from the Great Lakes Institute of Management & Illinois Institute of Technology. 

Hassan’s professional journey began at Accenture Solutions Pvt. Ltd., where he spent over two years honing his technical abilities – first as a Database Developer, ensuring the smooth operation of critical systems, and then as a Senior Developer specializing in SAP analytics. This experience laid the groundwork for his transition to a Data Science Consultant role at Deloitte USI, a position he held for over two years. 

At Deloitte, Hassan quickly proved his value, not only delivering advanced analytics but also taking the lead on project teams. He demonstrated a knack for building robust analytical infrastructures, evidenced by his work developing sentence correction functionality for a handwriting recognition system, and constructing analytics solutions on both SAP S/4 for financial reporting and expense forecasting, and Argus for accelerated querying and deeper insights. He’s a builder, a problem-solver, and a professional who consistently delivers tangible results through data-driven solutions.

India is a land steeped in history and burgeoning with modern ambition, a vibrant tapestry woven with ancient traditions and a rapidly evolving technological landscape. It’s a nation where the echoes of millennia-old empires resonate alongside the hum of innovation, a place where diverse cultures converge and contribute to a uniquely dynamic identity. A key player in the global economy, India serves as a significant hub for multinational corporations, including Accenture Solutions Pvt. Ltd., a prominent firm with a substantial presence within its borders. 

The nation’s technological prowess is increasingly evident in the careers forged within its cities, like Bengaluru in Karnataka, a focal point for engineering and IT professionals. Individuals like Abdelrahman Hassan, a graduate of New Horizon College of Engineering – affiliated with Visvesvaraya Technological University – exemplify this growth. Hassan’s journey, beginning with a Bachelor of Engineering in Electronics and Communication in 2013, led him to Accenture Solutions Pvt. Ltd. where he honed his skills, first as a Database Developer (2013-2014) and later as a Senior Developer specializing in SAP analytics (2014-2016). 

Furthering his expertise, Hassan pursued a PGDM in Business Analytics from Great Lakes Institute of Management & Illinois Institute of Technology (2017-2018), demonstrating India’s commitment to fostering advanced education and a skilled workforce. India, therefore, isn’t merely a geographical location; it’s a catalyst for professional development, a breeding ground for talent, and a vital component in the global network of technological advancement. It’s a nation actively shaping the future, one engineer, one analyst, one innovation at a time.

Accenture Solutions Pvt. Ltd., based in India, is a technology firm specializing in data analytics and application development. They are builders of intelligent systems, adept at translating business needs into robust, data-driven solutions. Between 2013 and 2016, the company demonstrated particular strength in SAP environments, notably developing the IPA analytics tool and introducing SAP Predictive Analytics – leveraging technologies like SAP HANA and R to unlock deeper insights. Their work on IPA involved meticulous database design, modeling, and the creation of key financial performance indicators. 

Beyond SAP, Accenture Solutions also managed the development and deployment of the ATAS tool, showcasing expertise in data flow analysis, database object creation, and SQL performance tuning, utilizing SQL Server (versions 2008 & 2014) and T-SQL. The firm’s technical toolkit is broad, encompassing C#, PL-SQL, and UML design, all supported by a Windows Server infrastructure and performance monitoring tools like SQL Server Profiler and Performance Monitor. Visual Studio 2010 features prominently in their development processes. 

Individuals like Abdelrahman Hassan, a Senior Developer specializing in SAP analytics and previously a Database Developer, exemplify the skilled personnel driving Accenture Solutions’ capabilities, contributing to both the analytical and foundational database layers of their projects. They are, at their core, architects of data, shaping raw information into actionable intelligence for their clients.

SAP HANA is a powerful, in-memory data platform – a digital core for modern, data-driven enterprises. It’s not merely a database; it’s a foundational layer enabling real-time analytics, application development, and intelligent technologies like machine learning and predictive modeling. Think of it as the high-performance engine beneath the hood, accelerating insights and powering innovation. 

Several organizations have harnessed its capabilities. Deloitte USI FMCG leveraged SAP HANA while building a Based Planning solution for Flowers Foods, establishing a robust foundation for CRM, CBP, and future forecasting – a testament to its ability to handle complex business processes. Accenture Solutions Pvt. Ltd. directly contributed to its analytical prowess, developing the IPA tool *with* SAP HANA, designing its database objects and data models, and integrating advanced features like predictive analytics. 

Individuals like Abdelrahman Hassan have also directly employed SAP HANA, building analytics infrastructures – first within SAP S/4 for financial reporting and expense forecasting at Deloitte USI, and later utilizing it as a core component for faster querying and advanced analytics. It’s a platform favored by those seeking to unlock the value hidden within data, a versatile tool for both large-scale enterprise solutions and focused analytical projects, and a key component in the modern data science toolkit alongside languages like Python, R, and C#, and visualization tools like Tableau.

R is a versatile and powerful language, favored by data scientists and statisticians for its robust analytical capabilities. Though quietly effective, R has been instrumental in streamlining complex business processes. Deloitte USI Telecom, for a brief but impactful period in early 2020, harnessed R’s power to build a decision tree model, dramatically reducing the tedious work of invoice classification – a 60% reduction in manual verification speaks to its efficiency. Accenture Solutions Pvt. Ltd. also recognized R’s potential, employing it alongside other technologies in their broader development of the SAP analytics tool (IPA) between 2014 and 2016.  R isn’t a standalone solution, however; it thrives in concert with a wider skillset. Those who wield R are often proficient in languages like Python and C#, and comfortable navigating databases like SAP HANA and MS SQL Server, visualizing insights with tools like Tableau, and applying advanced techniques in Machine Learning, Deep Learning, and Natural Language Processing.  It’s a language for those who seek to unlock meaning from data, a silent engine powering smarter decisions.

Python is a versatile and increasingly indispensable tool, a digital Swiss Army knife favored by data scientists, developers, and analysts alike. Not a being of scales and constriction, but a powerful programming language, it’s known for its clean syntax and readability – a quality that makes complex tasks surprisingly approachable. Recent projects at Deloitte USI demonstrate its practical strength: it served as the crucial bridge between the robust data storage of SAP HANA and the analytical power of K-means clustering, enabling detailed customer segmentation and automated categorization for FMCG clients. It’s a language comfortable handling large datasets, a skill honed through applications in Machine Learning, Deep Learning, and Natural Language Processing. 

Beyond the realm of big data, Python lends itself to more specialized applications. Abdelrahman Hassan, for instance, harnessed its capabilities to refine the accuracy of a handwriting recognition system, building functionality to correct sentence structure – a testament to its adaptability. While comfortable alongside other languages like R and C#, and tools like MS SQL Server and Tableau, Python often takes center stage, acting as the engine driving innovation in diverse fields. It’s a language that doesn’t just *process* information, it *understands* it, and empowers others to do the same.
